Check that a numeric PMF or weight vector is valid

Source: R/check.R
check_pmf_values.Rd

A numeric probability mass function or weight vector must be numeric, contain only finite, non-negative values, and not be all zero, so that it can be normalised to sum to one. Raises an informative error otherwise. Un-normalised vectors are allowed: they are treated as weights and normalised by the caller.

Usage

check_pmf_values(x, arg = "pmf")

Arguments

x

A numeric vector.

arg

The name of the calling argument, used in the error messages.

Value

x, invisibly, if it is valid; otherwise an error is raised.

On this page
